preamble: The diphthong /ai/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to /ou/ in specific linguistic contexts, notably observed in the morphophonological processes of some Japanese dialects.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves the backing, rounding, and raising of both elements of the diphthong

examples.note: Transformation of an i-adjective stem in Kansai dialect before polite forms like 'gozaimasu'.
Transformation of the i-adjective 'hayai' (early) in the polite greeting 'ohayou gozaimasu'.

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)
